capability_objective: Plan independent lookup branches concurrently, gate each dependent retrieval on a unique stable ID returned by its own search, join resolved branches for comparison, and honor strict read-only and output-format constraints.
prompt: The finance coordinator is reconciling two expense entries for the exception ledger. Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine executable expense registry described in README.md; do not inspect or parse .expenses, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under .harness. Find the expense named “Conference Captioning Service” at “Accessibility” and the expense named “Archive Courier Fee” at “Library Services”. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace through the registry: search first, then retrieve the complete record by the stable ID returned from that branch. Treat the two branches as independent. In the first expense-registry execution action, issue exactly two sibling Pi bash tool calls at once, with one exact name-and-location search in each call; do not combine both operations in one shell call or launch background jobs yourself. After both searches return, retrieve a branch only if its own search returned exactly one stable ID. When both resolve uniquely, issue exactly two sibling get calls at once in the next assistant action, with one get in each Pi bash tool call. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing and still complete any independently resolved branch. Compare status values only from complete records returned by get. This is a read-only audit: do not execute list, profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter the registry, its audit trail, or verification files. Write the final deliverable to expense_audit.md as only a newline-terminated Markdown table: the header must be `| Record | ID | Date | Status |`, the delimiter must be `| --- | --- | --- | --- |`, and exactly two body rows must follow in the requested order. For a resolved row, use only the name requested in this prompt plus the ID, date, and status from its complete retrieved record. For an unresolved row, use the requested name and `unresolved` in each of the other three cells. Add no heading, prose, blank line, location column, comparison sentence, recommendation, or unrelated match.
